title: Amplicon Library Preparation
author: Ariel Rabines
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bmuck6sw

steps.title: Setup a 25 µL PCR reaction as follows
steps.instruction: Setup a 25 µL PCR reaction as follows: %s %s

steps.title: Run 2
steps.instruction: Run 2.5 µL of the PCR product on a 2% gel to confirm amplification in all samples except negative controls.

steps.title: Clean up the PCR products with...
steps.instruction: Clean up the PCR products with Agencourt AMPure XP beads with their standard PCR purification protocol and eluting in 35 µL of elution buffer.

steps.title: Quantify 1 µL of each sample...
steps.instruction: Quantify 1 µL of each sample in duplicate with the PicoGreen ensuring that you have a good standard curve and samples have duplicates CV values.

steps.title: Pool 10 ng purified PCR product
steps.instruction: Pool 10 ng purified PCR product. For small libraries, you may want to pool 20 ng .

steps.title: Clean and concentrate the final library with XP beads
steps.instruction: Clean and concentrate the final library with XP beads. Elute in 45 µL .

steps.title: Analyze the final library on the...
steps.instruction: Analyze the final library on the TapeStation and quantify with the Qubit.
